evidence.text: flexibility when it comes to work life balance
work life balance and flexibility are great
benefits unlimited pto work life balance
pay increases decently yoy teams are nice enough decent work life balance extreme willingness to have employees move around to find what work works best for them pto is always respected
top tier clients work life balance pto

this experience will be valuable in future job opportunities as it has helped me develop specific skills or knowledge employers often seek candidates with a proven track record in relevant areas and this accomplishment demonstrates my ability to succeed in those areas additionally it enhances my problem-solving adaptability and leadership skills which are essential for career growth and advancement
prestige and reputation the big four firms are globally recognized and respected which can enhance your professional reputation and open doors for future career opportunities
strong brand reputation and career growth opportunities access to excellent resources and tools
learning and development career growth great networking opportinities
flexible exposure to clients responsibility good up-skill opportunities
unlimited pto and benefits good healthcare
pay benefits personal time off are very good
good pay time off networking
steady hours good pay unlimited pto
good pay and benefits that come with job

if you are willing to accept the inherent characteristics of working in big 4 audit stability hybrid work understaffed teams eating hours then kpmg is the right place to do it the people are all extremely kind and understanding of your learning curve and to an extent your personal life people in all practices and levels including managers and partners are very supportive and approachable

kpmg is a challenging workplace that will help you grow to help you understand the profession more you are often given opportunities to grow and try new things additionally they are supportive of you getting your cpa even providing becker materials the pay is fairly competitive and you have a chance to make great connections generally you are working with great peers who are quick to help if you are struggling to gr...
social events are the best
friendly environment and a lot to learn about
friendly environment and culture interactions with clients and coworkers great company benefits lakehouse training in florida every year
job security is really secure
- really supportive environment amongst coworkers
job security and stimulating work
great work culture and supportive environment
supportive environment you need to be a complete self driver
non-exempt do not qualify for unlimited pto no sick time aside from vacation time only accrue 15 pto days for the first few years these also have to be used for sick days
extremely low pay not the best benefits you will work with people who do the same work and make twice as much as you weird culture around firm contributions
hours are very long and constantly overworked it's a very strong burnout culture
lack of support for associates and managers mass layoffs handled poorly upper-level management does not support its managers and directors well
overworked underpaid burnout toxic undervalued
leadership has changed that made things bad they created offering structure but that is not working
salary is not competitive with market
if there is no work in the pipeline you will receive work from departments that are not in your expertise
work-life balance is not always practiced between teams
terrible work-life-balance don't have time to learn new thing only have time to roll-forward py after budget cut
lots of work during busy season some managers have an ego
toxic work environment lot of office politics
toxic family environment feel with little leadership
this is a very toxic place to work at least several years ago long work hours and stress that is hard to manage
toxic sales environment and undervalued
no real leadership constant layoffs constant reorganization meetings that talk about meetings no career growth no backing from leadership to make the right decision and often cave to the wrong ones
pay wasn't good at all
post covid culture and support is not the same as pre covid

no support for success no promotion even though you re fully utilized and they have you leading part of an engagement as an associate can end up working 40 hours a week on a project during busy season
a lot of time commitment minimal pay

- you will always be underpaid at any big 4 - motivation is cyclical depending on the season - will be a challenge if you are highly motivated because compensation will not be reflected by your additional efforts
project hours pay - raises and promotions are not that adequate
not much of an option for in-office collaboration
have to do a lot of compliance work as a jd llm hire salary is not a lot compared to law firms
very rigid and hierarchical employees are very attached to their title and like to go on power trips
